title: Pine Script v6 Exhaustive Language Reference Taxonomy
purpose: A detailed taxonomy for RAG systems that need to classify Pine Script v6 questions and chunks by namespace, invariant, built-in variable family, syntax construct, and language concept.

language_invariants.name: Bar-by-bar execution
language_invariants.definition: Pine scripts execute sequentially across chart bars, evaluating the script once for each historical bar and then on realtime updates according to script type and settings.

language_invariants.name: Implicit time-series history
language_invariants.definition: Values produced by expressions are stored as time series when their qualifier permits it, and prior values can be referenced with the history-referencing operator [].

language_invariants.name: Rollback and commit on realtime bars
language_invariants.definition: On realtime bars, Pine can roll variables and objects back to their last committed state on each update, then commit final states when the bar closes; declaration modes such as varip affect this behavior.

language_invariants.name: Qualifier hierarchy
language_invariants.definition: Qualified types progress from const to input to simple to series, and expressions generally inherit the strongest qualifier required by their operands or runtime dependencies.

language_invariants.name: Value and reference type split
language_invariants.definition: Fundamental value types hold direct values, while special drawing, collection, chart point, and UDT instances are reference IDs whose values are accessed or mutated through functions, methods, and fields.

language_invariants.name: Typed na values
language_invariants.definition: The na value represents an unavailable value; declarations initialized with na require an explicit type or a cast so the compiler can infer the intended type.

language_invariants.name: Global and local scope
language_invariants.definition: Top-level statements are in global scope, while functions, methods, conditionals, loops, and other blocks create local scopes that control visibility and mutation rules.

language_invariants.name: Single static script declaration
language_invariants.definition: A script is declared as exactly one indicator, strategy, or library using its declaration statement; that declaration determines runtime behavior and available outputs.

language_invariants.name: Visual side-effect model
language_invariants.definition: Visual outputs are produced by plot-family calls and drawing/table object APIs; many plot calls are designed for global-scope use, while drawing IDs can be created and mutated conditionally.

language_invariants.name: Runtime and object limits
language_invariants.definition: Scripts are subject to execution-time, request, memory, drawing-object, collection-size, and plot-count limits, so taxonomy metadata should preserve constructs that may affect limits.

syntax_taxonomy.definition: Compiler directive selecting Pine Script language version 6.
Single-line comments ignored by the compiler; also used by special annotations and compiler directives.
Top-level declaration that establishes the script kind and its compile/runtime properties.
Creates a variable, optionally specifying declaration mode and type before assigning an initial expression.
Destructures multiple returned values into identifiers; underscore discards unwanted values.
Updates an existing variable rather than declaring a new one.
Defines a user function with an implicit return value from the final expression or block result.
Defines a method callable with dot notation on values of the receiver type.
Declares a user-defined type with named fields and optional defaults.
Declares a set of symbolic values that can be used for type-safe choices and switch/input options.

syntax_taxonomy.syntax:
if condition
    block
else if condition
    block
else
    block
syntax_taxonomy.definition: Conditional structure that can execute side effects and return a value from branch-local blocks.
Multi-branch conditional structure that selects a result by expression value or by condition clauses.

syntax_taxonomy.syntax:
for i = start to end [by step]
    block
syntax_taxonomy.definition: Counted loop that iterates over an integer range with optional step.
Collection iteration loop for arrays, matrices, maps, and tuple-like item forms supported by the collection API.
Loop that repeats while the condition remains true.
References prior values in the expression's time series, where offset is typically a non-negative number of bars back.
Inline conditional expression that chooses one of two expressions.
Accesses methods on values and fields on user-defined objects or chart point references.
Imports an exported library namespace for use in a script.
Documentation annotations used to describe exported and user-facing script elements.

qualifiers.name: const
qualifiers.definition: Compile-time constant value that does not depend on inputs, chart data, or runtime state.

qualifiers.name: input
qualifiers.definition: Value determined by user input controls before script execution on bars.

qualifiers.name: simple
qualifiers.definition: Runtime value that is stable across bars after initialization or does not require full series history.

qualifiers.name: series
qualifiers.definition: Bar-dependent value that can vary across bars and can be history-referenced.

special_values_and_types.name: na
special_values_and_types.definition: Unavailable or undefined value requiring typed context in declarations.

special_values_and_types.name: void
special_values_and_types.definition: No usable return value, typically from side-effect functions.

reference_types.name: array<T>
reference_types.definition: Reference to a one-dimensional mutable collection of homogeneous elements.

reference_types.name: matrix<T>
reference_types.definition: Reference to a two-dimensional mutable collection of homogeneous elements.

reference_types.name: map<K,V>
reference_types.definition: Reference to a key-value collection with homogeneous key and value types.

reference_types.name: line
reference_types.definition: Drawing object reference for line drawings.

reference_types.name: linefill
reference_types.definition: Drawing object reference for fill areas between two lines.

reference_types.name: box
reference_types.definition: Drawing object reference for rectangular boxes.

reference_types.name: polyline
reference_types.definition: Drawing object reference for polyline drawings.

reference_types.name: label
reference_types.definition: Drawing object reference for text labels.

reference_types.name: chart.point
reference_types.definition: Reference-like point structure used by drawing APIs to carry bar/time and price coordinates.

reference_types.name: UDT
reference_types.definition: Instance of a user-defined type declared with the type keyword.

operator_taxonomy.definition: Numeric operators, with + also used for string concatenation where applicable.
Operators producing boolean comparisons between compatible values.
Boolean operators for negation, conjunction, and disjunction.
Ternary conditional operator for expression-level branching.
History-reference operator for retrieving prior time-series values.

namespace_taxonomy.name: array
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Functions and methods for creating, reading, writing, resizing, sorting, copying, and iterating one-dimensional collections.

namespace_taxonomy.name: box
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Functions and methods for rectangular drawing objects anchored to bar/time and price coordinates.

namespace_taxonomy.name: chart
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Chart-point helpers and chart metadata constructs used with drawing coordinates.

namespace_taxonomy.name: color
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Color construction and manipulation helpers.

namespace_taxonomy.name: hline
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Horizontal-line output helpers for static reference levels.

namespace_taxonomy.name: input
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Input functions that expose configurable values in the script settings UI.

namespace_taxonomy.name: label
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Functions and methods for text label drawing objects.

namespace_taxonomy.name: line
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Functions and methods for line drawing objects.

namespace_taxonomy.name: linefill
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Functions and methods for fills between two line objects.

namespace_taxonomy.name: log
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Logging helpers for debug output.

namespace_taxonomy.name: map
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Functions and methods for key-value collections.

namespace_taxonomy.name: math
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Mathematical constants and numeric functions.

namespace_taxonomy.name: matrix
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Functions and methods for two-dimensional collections.

namespace_taxonomy.name: plot
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Plot-related display and metadata helpers used by plot-family functions and fills.

namespace_taxonomy.name: polyline
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Functions and methods for polyline drawing objects defined by chart points.

namespace_taxonomy.name: request
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Cross-symbol, cross-timeframe, financial, economic, currency, seed, and lower-timeframe data request functions.

namespace_taxonomy.name: runtime
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Runtime error and execution-state helper namespace.

namespace_taxonomy.name: str
namespace_taxonomy.definition: String conversion, formatting, search, replacement, splitting, and casing helpers.

namespace_taxonomy.name: strategy
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Strategy backtesting, order-placement, risk, trade, position, and performance variables/functions.

namespace_taxonomy.name: syminfo
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Built-in variables describing the chart symbol, exchange, currency, pricescale, mintick, and instrument metadata.

namespace_taxonomy.name: ta
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Technical-analysis functions for moving averages, oscillators, volatility, correlation, pivots, bar conditions, and common indicator calculations.

namespace_taxonomy.name: table
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Functions and methods for floating table objects and cells.

namespace_taxonomy.name: ticker
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Ticker ID construction and modification helpers for sessions, adjustments, chart types, and special data feeds.

namespace_taxonomy.name: timeframe
namespace_taxonomy.definition: Built-in variables and helper functions for chart timeframe classification and conversion.

built_in_variable_taxonomy.definition: Current and derived OHLC price series for the chart symbol/timeframe.
Volume series for the chart symbol/timeframe where available.
Bar timestamps, current wall-clock-like time, and zero-based bar indices.
Boolean variables describing the current bar's execution state.
Boolean variables describing the current bar's session classification.
Variables describing chart colors and chart type.
Variables describing the currently charted instrument.
Strategy-level account, equity, profit, and position variables available in strategy scripts.
Trade collection namespaces exposing per-trade metrics for open and closed trades.

enum_and_constant_namespace_taxonomy.name: barmerge
enum_and_constant_namespace_taxonomy.definition: Constants controlling requested-series gap filling and lookahead behavior.

enum_and_constant_namespace_taxonomy.name: currency
enum_and_constant_namespace_taxonomy.definition: Currency code constants used in declarations, requests, and conversions.
members: currency constants such as USD, EUR, JPY and other supported ISO-like currency identifiers

enum_and_constant_namespace_taxonomy.name: display
enum_and_constant_namespace_taxonomy.definition: Constants controlling where visual outputs appear.

enum_and_constant_namespace_taxonomy.name: location
enum_and_constant_namespace_taxonomy.definition: Constants controlling shape/character/label placement relative to bars or panes.

enum_and_constant_namespace_taxonomy.name: position
enum_and_constant_namespace_taxonomy.definition: Constants controlling table position in the script pane or chart.

enum_and_constant_namespace_taxonomy.name: size
enum_and_constant_namespace_taxonomy.definition: Constants controlling visual object text/marker sizing.

enum_and_constant_namespace_taxonomy.name: strategy.direction
enum_and_constant_namespace_taxonomy.definition: Constants controlling allowed strategy direction in risk APIs.

enum_and_constant_namespace_taxonomy.name: strategy.oca
enum_and_constant_namespace_taxonomy.definition: Constants controlling one-cancels-all strategy order grouping.

enum_and_constant_namespace_taxonomy.name: strategy.order
enum_and_constant_namespace_taxonomy.definition: Constants controlling order alert behavior and fills where supported.
members: contract/order-related constants in the strategy namespace

enum_and_constant_namespace_taxonomy.name: text.align
enum_and_constant_namespace_taxonomy.definition: Constants controlling table and drawing text alignment.

variable_namespace_taxonomy.name: barstate
variable_namespace_taxonomy.definition: Execution-state booleans for the current bar.

variable_namespace_taxonomy.name: chart
variable_namespace_taxonomy.definition: Chart type and chart color metadata variables, plus chart point helpers.

variable_namespace_taxonomy.name: session
variable_namespace_taxonomy.definition: Session-state booleans for market, premarket, and postmarket bars.

variable_namespace_taxonomy.name: timeframe
variable_namespace_taxonomy.definition: Chart timeframe metadata variables and timeframe helper functions.

variable_namespace_taxonomy.name: strategy
variable_namespace_taxonomy.definition: Strategy state variables, order functions, risk functions, and trade collection namespaces.
